title: polypodium vulgare var. cambricum
intro: Polypodium vulgare var. cambricum, also known as the Welsh polypody or polypody fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Polypodium vulgare var. cambricum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, with herbal preparations often involving drying and infusing the leaves to create a medicinal infusion.
